verses.text: Compassed with 'so great a cloud of witnesses,' lay aside every weight and run the race, 'looking unto Jesus the author and finisher of our faith.' Run looking up. Fix your eyes on Christ, who endured the cross.

Compassed with 'so great a cloud of witnesses,' lay aside every weight and run the race, 'looking unto Jesus the author and finisher of our faith.' Run looking up. Fix your eyes on Christ, who endured the cross.
Consider Him who endured such hostility, lest you be wearied; you have 'not yet resisted unto blood.' Take heart. Your trials fall short of His.
Consider Him who endured such hostility, lest you be wearied; you have 'not yet resisted unto blood.' Take heart. Your trials fall short of His.
Despise not the Lord's chastening; 'whom the Lord loveth he chasteneth,' scourging every son He receives. Fatherly love. Discipline is a mark of sonship, not rejection.
Despise not the Lord's chastening; 'whom the Lord loveth he chasteneth,' scourging every son He receives. Fatherly love. Discipline is a mark of sonship, not rejection.
Endure chastening; God deals with you as sons, for what son is not chastened? Sure proof. The disciplined are owned as God's children.
Endure chastening; God deals with you as sons, for what son is not chastened? Sure proof. The disciplined are owned as God's children.

Earthly fathers corrected us; much more the Father of spirits, that we be 'partakers of his holiness.' Chastening yields 'the peaceable fruit of righteousness.' Fruitful pain. God's discipline ripens into peace and righteousness.

Earthly fathers corrected us; much more the Father of spirits, that we be 'partakers of his holiness.' Chastening yields 'the peaceable fruit of righteousness.' Fruitful pain. God's discipline ripens into peace and righteousness.

Earthly fathers corrected us; much more the Father of spirits, that we be 'partakers of his holiness.' Chastening yields 'the peaceable fruit of righteousness.' Fruitful pain. God's discipline ripens into peace and righteousness.
Lift up 'the hands which hang down,' and make straight paths, lest the lame be turned out of the way. Strengthen. Renewed courage helps the weak alongside.
Lift up 'the hands which hang down,' and make straight paths, lest the lame be turned out of the way. Strengthen. Renewed courage helps the weak alongside.
'Follow peace with all men, and holiness, without which no man shall see the Lord.' Beware the root of bitterness, and Esau's profane heart. Pursue holiness. Without it none shall see God.
'Follow peace with all men, and holiness, without which no man shall see the Lord.' Beware the root of bitterness, and Esau's profane heart. Pursue holiness. Without it none shall see God.
'Follow peace with all men, and holiness, without which no man shall see the Lord.' Beware the root of bitterness, and Esau's profane heart. Pursue holiness. Without it none shall see God.
'Follow peace with all men, and holiness, without which no man shall see the Lord.' Beware the root of bitterness, and Esau's profane heart. Pursue holiness. Without it none shall see God.
You are not come to the mount that 'burned with fire,' the terror of Sinai, where even Moses feared. Not Sinai. The old covenant came with dread.
You are not come to the mount that 'burned with fire,' the terror of Sinai, where even Moses feared. Not Sinai. The old covenant came with dread.
You are not come to the mount that 'burned with fire,' the terror of Sinai, where even Moses feared. Not Sinai. The old covenant came with dread.
You are not come to the mount that 'burned with fire,' the terror of Sinai, where even Moses feared. Not Sinai. The old covenant came with dread.
But to 'mount Sion... the city of the living God,' to Jesus 'the mediator of the new covenant,' and His sprinkled blood. Better mount. Grace brings us to joyful fellowship, not terror.
But to 'mount Sion... the city of the living God,' to Jesus 'the mediator of the new covenant,' and His sprinkled blood. Better mount. Grace brings us to joyful fellowship, not terror.
But to 'mount Sion... the city of the living God,' to Jesus 'the mediator of the new covenant,' and His sprinkled blood. Better mount. Grace brings us to joyful fellowship, not terror.
See that you refuse not Him that speaks; He will yet shake earth and heaven, that the unshaken may remain. Heed Him. Only what cannot be shaken will endure.
See that you refuse not Him that speaks; He will yet shake earth and heaven, that the unshaken may remain. Heed Him. Only what cannot be shaken will endure.
See that you refuse not Him that speaks; He will yet shake earth and heaven, that the unshaken may remain. Heed Him. Only what cannot be shaken will endure.
Receiving 'a kingdom which cannot be moved,' let us serve with reverence, 'for our God is a consuming fire.' Holy worship. Grace calls for awe before a holy God.
